title: Sweet Japanese Treacle Mochi
description: A smoky, flaky, crowd-pleaser Japanese Hokkaido dish featuring treacle, ideal for breakfast or snack time. It's fodmap-friendly and made with love.

ingredients.name: green tea
ingredients.notes: + green tea leaves for added flavor

instructions: Combine short-grain Japanese rice and kombu in a large saucepan with 500ml water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Drain the rice mixture and let it cool slightly.
Mix in treacle, black sugar, and matcha powder until well combined.
Add in the melted butter and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes on a lightly floured surface until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Shape the dough into small balls and flatten them slightly into disks.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and cook the mochi disks for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they're lightly golden and crispy.
Optional: serve with a side of green tea for an added touch of Japan.
Optional: sprinkle matcha powder on top for extra flavor.
